tasks.title: Scaffold packages/media package with tsup, ESM+CJS, and workspace registration
tasks.description:
Create the `packages/media` package following the `packages/icons` template.

Files to create:
- `packages/media/package.json` (name: `@repo/media`, type: module, tsup build, same scripts as icons)
- `packages/media/tsconfig.json` (extend shared TS config)
- `packages/media/src/index.ts` (barrel export file, initially empty)

Register in root `pnpm-workspace.yaml` if not auto-detected.

Deps to add: `cloudinary` (^2.9.0), `image-size`, `nanoid`
DevDeps: `tsup`, `vitest`, `typescript`

Verify: `pnpm install` succeeds, `pnpm --filter @repo/media build` succeeds with empty barrel.

Tests: No tests needed for scaffolding — verified by successful build.

subtasks.title: Run pnpm install and verify build

tasks.title: Implement resolveEnvironment() function with env detection logic
tasks.description:
Create `packages/media/src/utils/environment.ts`.

Implement `resolveEnvironment()` as a pure function that returns `'dev' | 'test' | 'preview' | 'prod'` based on:
- VERCEL_ENV=production -> prod
- VERCEL_ENV=preview -> preview
- NODE_ENV=test -> test
- else -> dev

Export from barrel.

Tests (`packages/media/src/utils/__tests__/environment.test.ts`):
- Returns 'prod' when VERCEL_ENV=production
- Returns 'preview' when VERCEL_ENV=preview
- Returns 'test' when NODE_ENV=test and no VERCEL_ENV
- Returns 'dev' when neither set
- VERCEL_ENV takes precedence over NODE_ENV

subtasks.title: Write unit tests for all env combinations

tasks.title: Define MEDIA_PRESETS constant with all 7 named transform presets
tasks.description:
Create `packages/media/src/presets.ts`.

Define `MEDIA_PRESETS` as a `readonly` Record mapping preset keys to Cloudinary transform strings:
- thumbnail: w_200,h_200,c_thumb,g_auto,q_auto,f_auto
- card: w_400,h_300,c_fill,g_auto,q_auto,f_auto
- hero: w_1200,h_600,c_fill,g_auto,q_auto,f_auto
- gallery: w_800,q_auto,f_auto
- avatar: w_150,h_150,c_thumb,g_face,q_auto,f_auto
- full: q_auto,f_auto
- og: w_1200,h_630,c_fill,q_auto,f_auto

Export `MediaPreset` type (keyof typeof MEDIA_PRESETS).

Tests (`packages/media/src/__tests__/presets.test.ts`):
- All 7 presets exist
- Each preset value is a non-empty string
- Object is frozen/readonly

tasks.title: Implement getMediaUrl() pure string transformation function
tasks.description:
Create `packages/media/src/get-media-url.ts`.

Implement `getMediaUrl(url: string | null | undefined, options?)` that:
- Cloudinary URL (contains 'res.cloudinary.com'): inserts transform string from preset between /upload/ and version/path
- Non-Cloudinary URL: returns unchanged
- Nullish/empty: returns fallback placeholder URL

Options: { preset?, width?, height?, raw? }
- preset: looks up MEDIA_PRESETS, throws TypeError for unknown
- width/height: override preset values
- raw: bypass presets, insert raw transform string

MUST NOT import cloudinary SDK. Pure string ops only.

Tests (`packages/media/src/__tests__/get-media-url.test.ts`):
- REQ-01.3-A: Cloudinary URL with preset 'card' inserts correct transforms
- REQ-01.3-B: Non-Cloudinary URL passes through unchanged
- REQ-01.3-C: null/undefined/empty returns fallback
- REQ-01.3-D: Width override replaces preset width
- REQ-01.3-E: Raw transform string bypasses presets
- REQ-01.4-A: Unknown preset throws TypeError
- URL with existing transforms gets new transforms inserted correctly
- Multiple presets produce correct transform strings

tasks.title: Implement extractPublicId() function for Cloudinary URL parsing
tasks.description:
Create `packages/media/src/extract-public-id.ts`.

Implement `extractPublicId(url: string | null | undefined): string | null` following the algorithm in REQ-01.5:
1. If URL doesn't contain res.cloudinary.com, return null
2. Find /upload/ segment
3. Split remaining path by /
4. Skip transform segments (contain commas)
5. Skip version segment (v followed by digits)
6. Join remaining with /
7. Remove file extension
8. Return public ID

Tests (`packages/media/src/__tests__/extract-public-id.test.ts`):
- REQ-01.5-A: Standard URL with version
- REQ-01.5-B: URL with transforms and version
- REQ-01.5-C: URL without version
- REQ-01.5-D: Non-Cloudinary URL returns null
- REQ-01.5-E: null/undefined/empty returns null
- URL with multiple transform segments
- URL with no extension

subtasks.title: Handle edge cases (no version, no extension)
Write unit tests for all scenarios

tasks.title: Implement validateMediaFile() for file size, MIME type, and dimension validation
tasks.description:
Create `packages/media/src/validate-media-file.ts`.

Implement `validateMediaFile({ buffer, mimeType, context })` where context is 'entity' | 'avatar'.

Validation rules:
- Entity: max size configurable (HOSPEDA_MEDIA_MAX_FILE_SIZE_MB, default 10), MIME: jpeg/png/webp/heic/avif, max dims: 8000x8000
- Avatar: max 5MB fixed, MIME: jpeg/png/webp, max dims: 4000x4000

Use `image-size` package to read dimensions from buffer headers.
Handle `image-size` parse failures as INVALID_IMAGE error (REQ-03.5-A).

Return typed result: `{ valid: true } | { valid: false, error: string, details: object }`

Error codes: FILE_TOO_LARGE, INVALID_FILE_TYPE, IMAGE_TOO_LARGE, INVALID_IMAGE

Tests (`packages/media/src/__tests__/validate-media-file.test.ts`):
- REQ-03-A: File too large rejected
- REQ-03-B: Disallowed MIME type rejected
- REQ-03-C: Avatar size limit (5MB)
- REQ-03-D: Valid file passes
- REQ-03-F: Oversized dimensions rejected
- REQ-03.5-A: Spoofed MIME type (image-size fails) rejected
- Edge: exactly at size limit passes
- Edge: exactly at dimension limit passes

subtasks.title: Implement size validation with configurable limit
Implement MIME type validation per context
Write tests with real image buffers for all scenarios

tasks.title: Define ImageProvider interface and implement CloudinaryProvider class
tasks.description:
Create `packages/media/src/provider/types.ts` and `packages/media/src/provider/cloudinary.provider.ts`.

ImageProvider interface methods:
- upload({ file: Buffer, folder, publicId?, tags?, overwrite? }) -> { url, publicId, width, height }
- delete({ publicId }) -> void
- deleteByPrefix({ prefix }) -> void
- buildUrl({ publicId, transforms? }) -> string

CloudinaryProvider:
- Constructor takes { cloudName, apiKey, apiSecret } (camelCase), maps to SDK snake_case
- Throws ConfigurationError if any credential missing
- upload() uses upload_stream() wrapped in Promise (see spec technical notes)
- Maps SDK secure_url to url in return type
- delete() calls cloudinary.uploader.destroy(), handles { result: 'not found' } without throwing
- deleteByPrefix() calls cloudinary.api.delete_resources_by_prefix()

Export generateGalleryId() wrapping nanoid(10).

Tests (`packages/media/src/provider/__tests__/cloudinary.provider.test.ts`):
- Mock cloudinary SDK (vi.mock('cloudinary'))
- REQ-01.1-A: upload returns correct shape
- REQ-01.1-B: delete calls destroy
- REQ-01.1-C: deleteByPrefix calls correct API
- REQ-01.2-A: Initializes from credentials
- REQ-01.2-B: Missing credentials throws ConfigurationError
- Upload maps secure_url, discards url
- Delete handles 'not found' without throwing
- upload_stream wrapping works with buffer

subtasks.title: Implement upload() with upload_stream Promise wrapper
Write mocked unit tests for all methods

tasks.title: Register 4 Cloudinary env vars in packages/config env registry
tasks.description:
Edit `packages/config/src/env-registry.hospeda.ts`.

Add 4 entries to HOSPEDA_ENV_VARS array:
1. HOSPEDA_CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME (string, not required, not secret, apps: ['api'], category: 'integrations')
2. HOSPEDA_CLOUDINARY_API_KEY (string, not required, secret: true, apps: ['api'], category: 'integrations')
3. HOSPEDA_CLOUDINARY_API_SECRET (string, not required, secret: true, apps: ['api'], category: 'integrations')
4. HOSPEDA_MEDIA_MAX_FILE_SIZE_MB (string, not required, not secret, default: '10', apps: ['api'], category: 'integrations', comment about Vercel Pro plan for >4.5MB)

Follow existing entry format exactly.

Update `.env.example` files for `apps/api` with the new vars.

Tests: Existing env registry tests should still pass. No new tests needed — env vars are validated at runtime.

tasks.title: Add upload request Zod schemas to @repo/schemas
Implement POST /api/v1/admin/media/upload endpoint
tasks.description:
Create `apps/api/src/routes/media/admin/upload.ts`.

Route: POST /api/v1/admin/media/upload (admin auth required)

Request: multipart/form-data with file, entityType, entityId, role

Flow:
1. Content-Length pre-check -> 413 if exceeds limit (REQ-03.4)
2. Parse multipart via ctx.req.formData()
3. Validate entityType/entityId/role via AdminUploadRequestSchema
4. Validate entityId is UUID (path traversal protection REQ-04.1-H)
5. Verify entity exists in DB via service lookup -> 404 if not found (REQ-04.1-G)
6. Convert File to Buffer via file.arrayBuffer()
7. Run validateMediaFile() -> 422 on failure
8. Build folder path: hospeda/{env}/{entityType}/{entityId}/{role or gallery/nanoid}
9. Call provider.upload() -> 502 on failure
10. Validate Cloudinary response has secure_url, public_id -> 502 if missing (REQ-04.1-I)
11. Return { url, publicId, width, height }

Cloudinary not configured -> 503 (REQ-06-B)

Mount route in admin route group.

Tests (`apps/api/src/routes/media/admin/__tests__/upload.test.ts`):
- Mock CloudinaryProvider
- REQ-04.1-A: Unauthenticated -> 401
- REQ-04.1-B: Successful upload returns correct shape
- REQ-04.1-C: Gallery generates nanoid suffix
- REQ-04.1-D: Missing field -> 422
- REQ-04.1-E: Cloudinary unreachable -> 502
- REQ-04.1-G: Entity not found -> 404
- REQ-04.1-H: Path traversal (non-UUID entityId) -> 422
- REQ-04.1-I: Incomplete Cloudinary response -> 502
- REQ-03-E: Content-Length too large -> 413
- REQ-06-B: No Cloudinary config -> 503

subtasks.title: Create route file with admin auth middleware
Implement entity existence check via DB
Wire file validation and Cloudinary upload
Write integration tests with mocked provider

tasks.title: Implement POST /api/v1/protected/media/upload avatar endpoint
tasks.description:
Create `apps/api/src/routes/media/protected/upload.ts`.

Route: POST /api/v1/protected/media/upload (any authenticated user)

Request: multipart/form-data with file only

Flow:
1. Content-Length pre-check -> 413 (avatar max 5MB)
2. Parse multipart
3. Extract userId from session
4. Convert File to Buffer
5. Run validateMediaFile({ buffer, mimeType, context: 'avatar' }) -> 422 on failure
6. Build folder: hospeda/{env}/avatars/{userId}
7. Call provider.upload({ overwrite: true }) -> 502 on failure
8. Return { url, publicId, width, height }

Cloudinary not configured -> 503

Mount in protected route group.

Tests (`apps/api/src/routes/media/protected/__tests__/upload.test.ts`):
- Mock CloudinaryProvider
- REQ-04.2-A: Authenticated upload succeeds with correct path
- REQ-04.2-B: Unauthenticated -> 401
- Avatar overwrite: true is passed to provider
- Avatar size limit enforced (5MB)
- Avatar MIME types enforced (no HEIC/AVIF)
- 503 when Cloudinary not configured

subtasks.title: Create route with protected auth middleware

tasks.title: Implement DELETE /api/v1/admin/media endpoint
tasks.description:
Create `apps/api/src/routes/media/admin/delete.ts`.

Route: DELETE /api/v1/admin/media?publicId={urlEncoded} (admin auth required)

Flow:
1. Parse and validate publicId query param via DeleteMediaQuerySchema
2. Validate publicId starts with 'hospeda/' (namespace protection)
3. Call provider.delete({ publicId })
4. Return { deleted: true, publicId } (HTTP 200)
5. Treat Cloudinary 'not found' same as success (idempotent)

Cloudinary not configured -> 503

Mount alongside upload in admin route group.

Tests (`apps/api/src/routes/media/admin/__tests__/delete.test.ts`):
- REQ-04.3-A: Successful delete
- REQ-04.3-B: publicId outside namespace -> 422
- REQ-04.3-C: Cloudinary not found -> still 200
- REQ-04.3-D: Unauthenticated -> 401
- REQ-04.3-E: Missing publicId -> 422
- 503 when Cloudinary not configured

subtasks.title: Create route with query param validation
Implement namespace check and delete call

tasks.title: Implement seed image cache system (read/write/invalidation)
tasks.description:
Create `packages/seed/src/utils/cloudinary-cache.ts`.

Implement:
- `readCache(cachePath)`: Read .cloudinary-cache.json, handle corrupted JSON (REQ-07.2-E)
- `writeCache(cachePath, cache)`: Atomic write (temp file + rename)
- `isCacheHit({ cacheEntry, currentUrl, currentFileMtime })`: Compare originalUrl or fileModifiedAt
- `updateCacheEntry(cache, publicId, { originalUrl, cloudinaryUrl, fileModifiedAt })`: Update single entry

Cache format per REQ-07.1.

Add `.cloudinary-cache.json` to `packages/seed/.gitignore`.

Tests (`packages/seed/src/utils/__tests__/cloudinary-cache.test.ts`):
- REQ-07.2-A: Cache miss when no file exists
- REQ-07.2-B: Cache hit when URL matches
- REQ-07.2-C: Cache miss when URL changed
- REQ-07.2-D: Cache miss when file mtime changed
- REQ-07.2-E: Corrupted cache is handled gracefully
- Atomic write creates temp file first

tasks.title: Implement seed image upload integration (fetch URL, upload to Cloudinary, update cache)
tasks.description:
Create `packages/seed/src/utils/cloudinary-upload.ts`.

Implement `uploadSeedImage({ originalUrl, entityType, entityId, role, galleryIndex?, provider, cache, env })`:
1. Build publicId: hospeda/{env}/seed/{entityType}/{entityId}/{role or gallery/index}
2. Check cache: if hit, return cached cloudinaryUrl
3. Fetch image from originalUrl (native fetch)
4. Upload buffer to Cloudinary via provider.upload()
5. Update cache with new entry
6. Return cloudinaryUrl

For gallery images in seed, use deterministic suffix based on array position (not nanoid) for cache stability.

Handle missing Cloudinary config gracefully (REQ-07.3): return original URL, log warning once.

Tests (`packages/seed/src/utils/__tests__/cloudinary-upload.test.ts`):
- Cache hit returns cached URL without upload
- Cache miss fetches and uploads
- Missing Cloudinary config returns original URL
- Gallery uses deterministic index-based suffix
- Failed fetch logs warning and returns original URL

subtasks.title: Implement publicId building for seed paths
Implement URL fetch and upload flow
Write unit tests with mocked provider and fetch

tasks.title: Integrate seed image upload into existing seed runners and add --clean-images CLI flag
tasks.description:

CLI changes:

- When --clean-images: call deleteByPrefix('hospeda/{env}/seed/') and delete cache file
- REQ-07.4-B: If Cloudinary not configured, only delete local cache, warn

Seed runner changes:
- Add `cleanImages` and `uploadImages` options to SeedOptions type
- In seed runners (runExampleSeeds, runRequiredSeeds), after loading each entity's seed data, call uploadSeedImage for each image URL
- Replace original URLs in seed data with Cloudinary URLs before DB insert
- REQ-07.3: If Cloudinary not configured, skip upload entirely, use original URLs

Tests:
- --clean-images calls deleteByPrefix and deletes cache
- --clean-images without Cloudinary only deletes local cache
- Seed run replaces URLs with Cloudinary URLs
- Seed run without Cloudinary uses original URLs

tasks.title: Create useMediaUpload hook for admin upload API calls
tasks.description:
Create `apps/admin/src/hooks/use-media-upload.ts`.

Implement `useMediaUpload()` hook that returns:
- `uploadEntityImage({ file, entityType, entityId, role })`: calls POST /api/v1/admin/media/upload with FormData, returns { url, publicId, width, height }
- `uploadAvatar({ file })`: calls POST /api/v1/protected/media/upload
- `deleteImage({ publicId })`: calls DELETE /api/v1/admin/media?publicId=...
- `isUploading`: boolean state
- `error`: error state
- `progress`: upload progress (if feasible with fetch)

Use existing TanStack Query mutation patterns from admin codebase.

Tests (`apps/admin/src/hooks/__tests__/use-media-upload.test.ts`):
- uploadEntityImage sends correct FormData
- uploadAvatar sends correct FormData
- deleteImage sends correct query param
- Error states are handled

tasks.title: Wire gallery image removal to delete API and add Cloudinary cleanup
tasks.description:
Edit `apps/admin/src/components/entity-form/fields/GalleryField.tsx`.

When an admin removes a gallery image:
1. Extract publicId from the image URL using extractPublicId() from @repo/media
2. If publicId is not null (Cloudinary URL), call useMediaUpload().deleteImage({ publicId })
3. Remove the image from gallery array in form state
4. If publicId is null (non-Cloudinary URL), just remove from array (no API call)

REQ-08.2-B: Gallery removal triggers delete endpoint.
REQ-08.2-C: Featured image replacement uses overwrite (no separate delete needed).

Tests:
- Removing Cloudinary gallery image calls delete API
- Removing non-Cloudinary image skips delete API
- Gallery reorder does not trigger deletes
- Error from delete API is handled gracefully (image still removed from UI)

subtasks.title: Wire remove action to delete API

tasks.title: Refactor apps/web/src/lib/media.ts to use getMediaUrl() from @repo/media
tasks.description:

1. Import getMediaUrl from @repo/media
2. Update extractFeaturedImageUrl to accept optional preset param (default 'card')
3. After extracting raw URL via existing fallback logic, pass through getMediaUrl(url, { preset })
4. Update extractGalleryUrls to accept optional preset param (default 'gallery')
5. Map each gallery URL through getMediaUrl(url, { preset })
6. Remove any local URL transformation logic if present

REQ-10.1-A: Non-Cloudinary URLs pass through unchanged, visual output identical.

Tests (`apps/web/src/lib/__tests__/media.test.ts`):
- Unsplash URL returned unchanged through getMediaUrl passthrough
- Cloudinary URL gets preset transforms applied
- Fallback chain still works

tasks.title: Add Cloudinary cleanup hooks to entity services (accommodation, destination, event, post)
tasks.description:
Edit entity service files to add hard delete image cleanup.

REQ-12.3: Use _beforeHardDelete/_afterHardDelete lifecycle hooks.

For each service (accommodation, destination, event, post):
1. Override `_beforeHardDelete(id, actor, ctx)`: store id and entityType in ctx.hookState
2. Override `_afterHardDelete(result, actor, ctx)`: if result.count > 0, call provider.deleteByPrefix()
3. Catch errors in afterHardDelete — log warning, do NOT re-throw (best-effort)

Files:
- `packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation/accommodation.service.ts`
- `packages/service-core/src/services/destination/destination.service.ts` (or wherever it is)
- `packages/service-core/src/services/event/event.service.ts`
- `packages/service-core/src/services/post/post.service.ts`

For user avatar cleanup: override in user service to call provider.delete() with single avatar publicId.

REQ-12.2: Soft delete does NOT trigger cleanup.

Tests:
- REQ-12-A: Hard delete calls deleteByPrefix with correct path
- REQ-12-B: Soft delete does NOT call any Cloudinary method
- REQ-12-C: Cloudinary failure does not block entity deletion
- REQ-12-D: User hard delete calls delete (not deleteByPrefix)

tasks.title: End-to-end verification: no direct cloudinary imports outside @repo/media
tasks.description:
Run a codebase-wide grep to verify acceptance criterion 15: 'No app or package other than packages/media imports from the cloudinary SDK.'

Checks:
1. grep for `from 'cloudinary'` or `require('cloudinary')` outside packages/media -> must find 0 results
2. grep for `import.*cloudinary` outside packages/media -> must find 0 results
3. Verify getMediaUrl is used (not raw URLs) in admin and web img tags
4. Verify all upload/delete routes return correct response shapes
5. Run full typecheck: `pnpm typecheck`
6. Run full lint: `pnpm lint`
7. Run all tests: `pnpm test`

Document any violations found and fix them.

This is a validation task — no new code, just verification.

subtasks.title: Grep for cloudinary imports outside packages/media
